body.title: Version-controlled seed configs — watchlist + machines + any other lost-and-rebuilt state
body.result: Shipped: lab/seed/watchlist.yaml + lab/seed/machines.yaml (versioned source of truth). Seeders: seed_watchlist.py reads YAML with fallback; seed_machines.py reads YAML, upserts via ON CONFLICT. Pattern doc at lab/design_docs/seed_config_versioning.md. Verified both round-trip cleanly. Commit a08daee1.
body.description:
Akien 2026-04-18b: 'we lost the machine info and we lost the questions. maybe those are two things needing versioning?' Both the machines table and the watchlist (GH-299) drifted or got wiped without a rollback path, and both were rebuilt from memory. The missing pattern is durable version control for config-shaped state.

## Principle
Anything that is (a) config-shaped (small, structured), (b) load-bearing for Igor's cognition, and (c) hand-curated rather than graph-accreted should live as a seed file in git, with idempotent boot-time upsert. Git log IS the version history; rollback is git checkout + re-seed.

## Candidates
- lab/seed/watchlist.yaml — 9 questions + 9 topics Akien dictated 2026-04-18 (currently hardcoded in seed_watchlist.py — move to YAML)
- lab/seed/machines.yaml — hostname, ip, os, ram_gb, network_type, ollama_model, roles, aliases, ssh_enabled, notes. Currently only in Postgres; nothing in repo.
- Potential future: CP cornerposts, identity patterns, clan-sheet seeds (if not already in git elsewhere)

## Scope for this ticket
1. Create lab/seed/watchlist.yaml — 18 entries (9 questions + 9 topics) from seed_watchlist.py
2. Refactor seed_watchlist.py to read from YAML instead of hardcoded lists
3. Create lab/seed/machines.yaml — snapshot of current machines table
4. Create lab/seed/seed_machines.py — idempotent upsert from YAML → machines table
5. Document the pattern in lab/design_docs/seed_config_versioning.md

## Non-goals
- Moving every DB row into YAML (graph state stays in DB; only config-shaped seeds move)
- Live-editing YAML → DB sync (Phase 1 is boot-time only)
- A full migration framework (already have _migrations for schema; this is data, not schema)

## Acceptance
- `git log lab/seed/watchlist.yaml` shows the 2026-04-18 restore as a commit
- `python3 lab/claudecode/seed_watchlist.py` reads YAML, upserts 18 habits, idempotent on re-run
- Same shape for machines: re-seeding is a one-liner that can recover from DB wipe

## Provenance
Akien-Claude 2026-04-18b, parting ask. Two lost-and-rebuilt incidents in one session triggered the insight.
